task_text: Prepare a comprehensive project health briefing for NovaBridge's leadership team. The briefing should cover: current progress, key risks, upcoming Q3 commitments, and any issues that need leadership attention. Save it as project_health_briefing.txt.

Project Helios — Weekly Status Update
Week of 2026-07-06

Overall: ON TRACK (minor risks)

Milestone Progress:
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered)
- Client dashboard: 60% complete
- Integration testing: 40% complete

Sprint 14 Goals:
- Complete dashboard module v1
- Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
- Begin UAT preparation

Open Blockers:
1. Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs. MM/DD/YYYY).
   Dev team awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. Estimated delay: 2-3 days.
2. Third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal pending. Legal reviewing.
   Target resolution: 2026-07-14.

Next milestone: Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25

Project Helios — Q2 Deliverables Checklist

[DONE]      Requirements documentation v1.0
[DONE]      System architecture design
[DONE]      API gateway implementation
[DONE]      Development environment setup
[DONE]      Data schema finalization
[IN-PROGRESS] Data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
[IN-PROGRESS] Client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
[OVERDUE]   Integration test suite setup (was due 2026-06-30; rescheduled to 2026-07-20)
[OVERDUE]   UAT environment provisioning (was due 2026-07-01; blocked by Meridian IT access)
[OVERDUE]   Performance benchmarking report (was due 2026-07-05; owner: Derek Okafor)

Project Helios — Q3 Roadmap
INTERNAL DRAFT — Not for client distribution
Last updated: 2026-07-06

Q3 2026 Priorities:

1. COMPLETE SYSTEM INTEGRATION (July)
   - Finalize data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
   - Complete client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
   - Integration test sign-off (target: 2026-07-25)

2. PRODUCT DEMO FOR MERIDIAN (July 25)
   - Live data ingestion demonstration using Meridian sample data
   - End-to-end pipeline walkthrough
   - Dashboard visualization with real data (per contract Section 9.3)

3. USER ACCEPTANCE TESTING — MERIDIAN GROUP (August 17-28)
   - Deploy to UAT environment
   - Support Meridian user testing sessions
   - Address UAT feedback (budget: 2 sprints)

4. PERFORMANCE HARDENING (August-September)
   - Dashboard load time target: <2 seconds for 100k records
   - Ingestion throughput benchmarking (target: 500k records/min sustained)
   - External security penetration test (vendor TBD)

5. LAUNCH PREPARATION (September)
   - Production environment setup and runbook documentation
   - Hypercare plan finalization (30-day post-launch model)
   - Final client training sessions

Target launch date: 2026-10-01
Key risk: UAT slippage of >2 weeks pushes launch into Q4, triggering Section 7.1 penalty review.

Proposed Q3 feature additions (subject to change control approval):
- Scheduled ingestion jobs (cron-based triggers)
- Email alerting for pipeline failures
- Self-service mapping rule editor (deferred from Q2)

Project Helios — Risk Register
Last reviewed: 2026-07-07 | Owner: Sarah Chen (PM)

ID    Risk Description                             Likelihood  Impact  Score  Mitigation Owner    Status
----  -------------------------------------------  ----------  ------  -----  ------------------  --------
R-01  Meridian source data quality issues           High        High    9      Amir Hassan         Active — date format blocker confirmed in sprint 14
R-02  GeoSync license not renewed before demo       Medium      High    6      Raj Patel           Active — legal review in progress, due 2026-07-14
R-03  Dashboard performance on large datasets       Medium      High    6      Derek Okafor        Active — benchmark task HEL-247 not yet started
R-04  UAT timeline slippage                        High        Medium  6      Sarah Chen          Active — 1-week extension already consumed
R-05  Key person dependency: Derek Okafor           Low         High    3      Sarah Chen          Monitored — no backup tech lead identified
R-06  Meridian IT environment access delays        Medium      Medium  4      Nadia Petrov        Active — awaiting IT infrastructure diagram
R-07  Scope creep from Meridian feature requests   Low         Medium  2      Raj Patel           Monitored — 3 informal requests logged, not yet escalated
R-08  Cloud cost overrun                           Low         Low     1      Nadia Petrov        Monitored
R-09  Third-party API deprecation (non-GeoSync)    Very Low    Medium  1      Olivia Brooks       Monitored

Meeting Notes: Project Helios / Meridian Group Sync
Date: 2026-07-08
Attendees: Sarah Chen (NovaBridge PM), Derek Okafor (NovaBridge Tech Lead),
           Priya Kapoor (Meridian IT Lead), James Whitfield (Meridian Project Sponsor)

Decisions:
1. Demo date confirmed: 2026-07-25 (product demo for Meridian stakeholders)
2. Meridian will provide clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15 to unblock date format issue
3. UAT period extended by 1 week (now Aug 17-28)

Action Items:
- NovaBridge: Resolve GeoSync license before demo (owner: Derek Okafor, due 2026-07-14)
- NovaBridge: Send draft demo agenda to Meridian by 2026-07-18 (owner: Sarah Chen)
- Meridian: Share IT infrastructure diagram for integration environment setup
  (owner: Priya Kapoor, due 2026-07-12)

Notes:
- James Whitfield emphasized that the demo must include live data ingestion, not mock data.
- Concern raised about dashboard load times on large datasets. Derek to benchmark before demo.
- Priya Kapoor confirmed Meridian IT will provision UAT environment access by 2026-07-19.

input.content:
NovaBridge Project Health Briefing — Project Helios
Prepared for: NovaBridge Leadership Team
Date: 2026-07-11

Executive Summary
Project Helios is currently assessed as ON TRACK with minor but time-sensitive risks. Core platform foundations are in place: requirements, architecture, API gateway, development environment, and data schema are complete. Remaining July work is concentrated around finalizing data ingestion, completing dashboard v1, standing up integration/UAT readiness, and preparing for the Meridian product demo on 2026-07-25.

Leadership should pay particular attention to four areas: Meridian data quality dependency, GeoSync license renewal, dashboard performance benchmarking, and UAT environment/access readiness. Any slippage greater than two weeks in UAT could push the launch into Q4 and trigger a Section 7.1 penalty review.

1. Current Progress

Overall Status
- Overall project status: ON TRACK, with minor risks.
- Next major milestone: Integration Test Sign-off targeted for 2026-07-25.
- Product demo for Meridian stakeholders confirmed for 2026-07-25.
- Target launch date remains 2026-10-01.

Milestone Completion
- Requirements documentation v1.0: Complete.
- System architecture design: Complete.
- API gateway: Complete and delivered.
- Development environment setup: Complete.
- Data schema finalization: Complete.
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete; target completion 2026-07-18.
- Client dashboard v1: 60% complete; target completion 2026-07-22.
- Integration testing: 40% complete; sign-off target 2026-07-25.

Q2 Deliverables Status
- Completed: Requirements, architecture, API gateway, development environment, and data schema.
- In progress: Data ingestion module and client dashboard v1.
- Overdue items:
  - Integration test suite setup: originally due 2026-06-30; rescheduled to 2026-07-20.
  - UAT environment provisioning: originally due 2026-07-01; blocked by Meridian IT access.
  - Performance benchmarking report: originally due 2026-07-05; owner Derek Okafor.

Sprint 14 Focus
- Complete dashboard module v1.
- Resolve data format inconsistencies.
- Begin UAT preparation.

2. Key Risks

High-Priority Active Risks

R-01: Meridian source data quality issues
- Likelihood: High; Impact: High; Score: 9.
- Current issue: Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats, including ISO 8601 and MM/DD/YYYY.
- Status: Active blocker in Sprint 14.
- Mitigation: Meridian committed to providing a clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15.
- Leadership concern: If clean sample data is delayed, it may compromise the live ingestion requirement for the 2026-07-25 demo.

R-02: GeoSync license renewal before demo
- Likelihood: Medium; Impact: High; Score: 6.
- Status: Active; legal review in progress.
- Target resolution: 2026-07-14.
- Leadership concern: Demo functionality may be at risk if the third-party mapping library license is not renewed in time.

R-03: Dashboard performance on large datasets
- Likelihood: Medium; Impact: High; Score: 6.
- Status: Active; benchmarking task HEL-247 has not yet started.
- Target Q3 performance commitment: Dashboard load time under 2 seconds for 100k records.
- Leadership concern: Meridian raised concern about dashboard load times, and the demo is expected to use real data.

R-04: UAT timeline slippage
- Likelihood: High; Impact: Medium; Score: 6.
- Status: Active; one-week UAT extension already consumed.
- Current UAT window: 2026-08-17 to 2026-08-28.
- Leadership concern: Slippage greater than two weeks could push launch into Q4 and trigger Section 7.1 penalty review.

Additional Monitored/Active Risks
- R-05: Key person dependency on Derek Okafor; no backup tech lead identified.
- R-06: Meridian IT environment access delays; awaiting IT infrastructure diagram, due from Meridian by 2026-07-12.
- R-07: Scope creep from Meridian feature requests; three informal requests logged, not yet escalated.
- R-08: Cloud cost overrun; currently low likelihood and low impact.
- R-09: Non-GeoSync third-party API deprecation; very low likelihood, medium impact.

3. Upcoming Q3 Commitments

July: Complete System Integration
- Finalize data ingestion module by 2026-07-18.
- Complete client dashboard v1 by 2026-07-22.
- Complete integration test sign-off by 2026-07-25.

July 25: Product Demo for Meridian
- Demo date confirmed for 2026-07-25.
- Required demo content:
  - Live data ingestion demonstration using Meridian sample data.
  - End-to-end pipeline walkthrough.
  - Dashboard visualization with real data, per contract Section 9.3.
- Meridian sponsor emphasized that the demo must include live data ingestion, not mock data.
- NovaBridge action item: Send draft demo agenda to Meridian by 2026-07-18; owner Sarah Chen.

August 17-28: Meridian User Acceptance Testing
- Deploy to UAT environment.
- Support Meridian user testing sessions.
- Address UAT feedback with a planned budget of two sprints.
- Meridian IT has indicated UAT environment access will be provisioned by 2026-07-19.

August-September: Performance Hardening
- Dashboard load time target: under 2 seconds for 100k records.
- Ingestion throughput target: 500k records per minute sustained.
- External security penetration test; vendor still to be determined.

September: Launch Preparation
- Production environment setup.
- Runbook documentation.
- Hypercare plan finalization for a 30-day post-launch model.
- Final client training sessions.

Target Launch
- Target launch date: 2026-10-01.

Proposed Q3 Feature Additions Requiring Change Control Approval
- Scheduled ingestion jobs using cron-based triggers.
- Email alerting for pipeline failures.
- Self-service mapping rule editor, deferred from Q2.

4. Issues Requiring Leadership Attention

1. Ensure GeoSync license renewal is resolved by 2026-07-14
- Why it matters: The mapping library is needed before the 2026-07-25 demo.
- Requested leadership support: Monitor legal review and remove escalation blockers if renewal is not complete by the target date.

2. Protect the 2026-07-25 demo from data-quality dependency risk
- Why it matters: Meridian requires live data ingestion, not mock data, and clean sample data is due from Meridian on 2026-07-15.
- Requested leadership support: Be prepared to escalate with Meridian leadership if clean sample data is not received on time.

3. Accelerate dashboard performance benchmarking
- Why it matters: The benchmarking report is overdue, the related task has not started, and Meridian has already raised concerns about large-dataset load times.
- Requested leadership support: Confirm ownership and priority with the technical lead; consider adding backup engineering support given the dependency on Derek Okafor.

4. Monitor UAT environment readiness and access
- Why it matters: UAT provisioning is overdue and dependent on Meridian IT. UAT has already been extended by one week.
- Requested leadership support: Track Meridian delivery of the IT infrastructure diagram due 2026-07-12 and UAT access expected by 2026-07-19.

5. Address key-person dependency
- Why it matters: Derek Okafor owns or is central to several critical items, including benchmarking, technical leadership, and GeoSync follow-through.
- Requested leadership support: Assign a backup technical lead or additional senior engineer to reduce delivery risk during July and August.

6. Enforce change control for proposed Q3 feature additions
- Why it matters: The project is entering integration, UAT, and launch preparation; unapproved additions could worsen UAT slippage and threaten launch timing.
- Requested leadership support: Require formal approval for scheduled ingestion jobs, email alerts, and self-service mapping rule editor before work begins.

5. Bottom Line

Project Helios remains viable against the 2026-10-01 launch date, but the July execution window is critical. The most urgent leadership focus should be on resolving the GeoSync license, securing clean Meridian sample data, starting overdue performance benchmarking, and ensuring UAT environment readiness. If these items are controlled over the next two weeks, the project should remain on track for Q3 commitments and the October launch.
